surface_forms: the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center
description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

surface_forms: one of the towers of the World Trade Center
description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

surface_forms: my office in Building 136 in the Washington Navy Yard
description: former shipyard and ordnance plant of the United States Navy
description_wiki: The Washington Navy Yard (WNY) is a ceremonial and administrative center for the United States Navy, located in the federal national capital city of Washington, D.C. (federal District of Columbia).

description: international financial institution
international financial institution
description_wiki: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is an international financial institution and a specialized agency of the United Nations, headquartered in Washington, D.C. It consists of 191 member countries, and its stated mission is "working to foster global monetary cooperation, secure financial stability, facilitate international trade, promote high employment and sustainable economic growth, and reduce poverty around the world".

description: American news channel
description_wiki: Cable News Network (CNN) is an American multinational  news media company and the flagship namesake property of CNN Worldwide, a division of Warner Bros.

description: federal district of the United States of America, containing the capital city of the United States, Washington

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

surface_forms: the area around the White House
description: official residence and office of the President of the United States
description_wiki: The White House is the official residence and workplace of the President of the United States.

surface_forms: the area around the Washington Monument
description: obelisk on the National Mall in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The Washington Monument is a 555-foot (169 m) tall obelisk on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., built to commemorate George Washington, a Founding Father of the United States and the nation's first president.

description: memorial in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Lincoln Memorial is  a U.S. national memorial honoring Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the United States, located on the western end of the National Mall of Washington, D.C. The memorial is built in a neoclassical style in the form of a classical temple.

description: memorial in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Thomas Jefferson Memorial is a national memorial in Washington, D.C., built in honor of Thomas Jefferson, the principal author of the United States Declaration of Independence and the nation's third president.

description: Washington, D.C. rapid transit system
description_wiki: The Washington Metro, often abbreviated as the Metro and formally the Metrorail, is a rapid transit system serving the Washington metropolitan area of the United States.

description: former telecommunications company

aliases: Rock Creek and Potomac Parkway Historic District
description: parkway in Washington D.C.
bridge in Washington, D.C., United States of America
description_wiki: The Theodore Roosevelt Bridge (also known as the Teddy Roosevelt Bridge, Roosevelt Bridge, or T.R. Bridge) is a bridge crossing the Potomac River which connects Washington, D.C., with the Commonwealth of Virginia.

description: bridge connecting Washington, D.C., and Arlington, Virginia
description_wiki: The Arlington Memorial Bridge (often shortened to Memorial Bridge) is a Neoclassical masonry, steel, and stone arch bridge that crosses the Potomac River in Washington, D.C., the capital of the United States.

description: organization
description_wiki: Metropolitan Washington Council of Governments (MWCOG) is an independent, nonprofit association designed to address regional issues confronting Washington, D.C., suburban Maryland, and Northern Virginia.

description: national cemetery in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Arlington National Cemetery is the largest cemetery in the United States National Cemetery System, one of two maintained by the United States Army.

description: state highway in Arlington County, Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Virginia State Route 110 (SR 110) is a primary state highway in Arlington, Virginia.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

entity: United States Secretary of the Interior
description: head of the Department of the Interior in the United States government
description_wiki: The United States secretary of the interior is the head of the United States Department of the Interior.

description: uniformed federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.

aliases: the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
description: former country in Eurasia (1922–1991)
description_wiki: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), also known as the Soviet Union, was a transcontinental country that spanned much of Eurasia from 1922 until its dissolution in 1991.
